sound_type: growling guttural colony roar -- deep, grating 'arrr' from individuals, overwhelming wall of sound from colony of thousands. E=5
ecosystem: Rocky cliff faces, offshore islands, open ocean. Breeds on narrow cliff ledges in extremely dense colonies. Winters at sea.
season: Summer breeder at coastal colonies. Winters at sea. Present on PNW coast year-round but colonies active spring-summer.

coordinates.description: Common Murre on PNW coastal cliff colonies. Oregon and Washington offshore rocks.

taxonomy_note.description: FOURTH ALCIDAE (174-177). Continuing Alcidae streak. Dense cliff colony specialist. Pear-shaped egg adaptation. No nest -- egg on bare rock. Deep diver (100+ meters).
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Alcidae: Pigeon Guillemot (174), Rhinoceros Auklet (175), Tufted Puffin (176), Common Murre (177).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Uria from Greek 'ouria' -- water bird. aalge from Swedish/Danish name for murre/auk. 'Murre' possibly from the growling call sound.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Uria -- murres. 2 species (Common Murre and Thick-billed Murre). Dense colony cliff nesters. Deep divers.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Charadriiformes. Alcidae continuing from degrees 174-176. Fourth consecutive entry.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Fourth Alcidae. First Uria. Colony specialist with pear-shaped egg. Streak continues.

song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual call: 0.3-1.0 seconds. Colony roar: continuous.
song_metrics.note_structure: Deep growling 'arrr' -- guttural, grating. Individual moderate. Colony overwhelming.
song_metrics.note_types: Colony roar (primary -- breeding), alarm call (higher pitched), chick call (high peeping).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Individual: repeated growling calls. Colony: continuous overlapping wall of sound from thousands.
song_metrics.timbre: Guttural, grating, resonant. Low-frequency. Growling quality. Immersive in colony context.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=5. Individual moderate. Colony of thousands = overwhelming. Multiplication effect.
song_metrics.tempo: Individual calls at ~1-3 per second. Colony continuous.
song_metrics.context: Breeding colonies on cliff ledges. Summer. Contact, alarm, territorial.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Colony sound consistent across range.
song_metrics.key_property: COLONY ROAR -- growling, guttural, wall of sound from thousands. Pear-shaped egg. Dense cliff colonies. E=5. v1.49.317.

plumage.overall: Breeding: black head, back, wings. White underparts. Thin dark bill. Non-breeding: white extends up onto cheeks/throat. Black-and-white pattern throughout.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: Penguin-like upright posture. Black-and-white. Thin pointed bill. Dense colony on cliff ledges.
plumage.seasonal_variation: Breeding: all-black head. Non-breeding: white cheek patch. Bill remains dark and pointed.
plumage.flight_pattern: Fast, direct, low over water. Rapid wingbeats. Small wings relative to body (trade-off for underwater flight).

physical_description.overall: Length: 38-43 cm. Weight: 900-1100g. Bill: dark, thin, pointed. Penguin-like stance.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Upright penguin-like posture. Black-and-white. Thin bill. Dense colony context.
physical_description.field_id: Medium-large black-and-white seabird on cliff ledges. Upright posture. Dense colony. Listen for growling colony roar. Compare with Thick-billed Murre (not in PNW commonly).

nodes.title: Colony Roar -- Collective Sound Wall
nodes.description: Individual growling 'arrr' moderate volume. Multiplied by thousands in dense colony = overwhelming, continuous wall of sound. E=5 through multiplication.

nodes.title: Pear-Shaped Egg -- Anti-Roll Adaptation
nodes.description: Strongly pyriform egg rolls in circle, not off ledge. Extraordinary color variation for parent recognition. One egg per attempt. Incubated on feet.

nodes.title: Densest Colonies -- 20 Pairs per Square Meter
nodes.description: Among densest breeding colonies of any bird. Shoulder to shoulder on narrow ledges. No nest. Density as anti-predator strategy.

nodes.title: Chick Cliff Departure
nodes.description: Flightless chicks leap from cliff ledges (sometimes 300+ feet) at 15-25 days old. Male accompanies at sea for weeks. Dramatic life history event.

cross_references.relationship: ALCIDAE STREAK! Both cliff nesters. Both Alcidae. Puffin: burrow. Murre: open ledge. Share colony sites. Different nesting strategies.
ALCIDAE STREAK! Auklet: nocturnal burrow. Murre: diurnal cliff. Both deep divers. Different temporal and spatial niches within Alcidae.
ALCIDAE OPENS! Guillemot: nearshore crevice. Murre: offshore cliff. Both black-and-white. Alcidae range from nearshore to pelagic.
ALCIDAE CONTINUES! Murrelet: old-growth forest nester. Murre: cliff nester. Most extreme nesting habitat contrast in any bird family.
